title: viola lutea subsp. caespitosa
intro: Viola lutea subsp. caespitosa, also known as the yellow violet or alpine pansy, is a perennial plant species in the viola genus, characterized by its bright yellow flowers and heart-shaped leaves. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Viola lutea subsp. caespitosa are attributed to its ability to reduce stress, improve digestion, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. Herbal preparations of Viola lutea subsp. caespitosa include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to support overall well-being and promote relaxation.
